playbook.summary: Bank linking issues are usually caused by incorrect credentials, unsupported institutions, or a temporary Plaid outage.
steps: Confirm you are using your online banking username and password (not your app PIN).
Check that your bank is on our supported institutions list at nexafi.com/supported-banks.
Try unlinking and re-linking your account from Settings > Linked Accounts.
If the issue persists, check the NexaFi status page for any ongoing outages.
Contact support at support@nexafi.com with your account email and the error message you see.
playbook.follow_up_question: Can you tell me which bank you are trying to connect and what error message you are seeing?

playbook.summary: ACH transfers typically take 1-3 business days. Instant deposits may be delayed by bank-side holds.
steps: Check your transaction history under Portfolio > Activity.
ACH transfers take 1-3 business days to settle.
Instant deposit eligibility depends on your bank and account history.
If it has been more than 3 business days, contact support with your transfer confirmation number.
playbook.follow_up_question: When did you initiate the deposit and which transfer method did you use (ACH or instant)?

playbook.summary: Login issues are usually resolved by resetting your password or clearing your browser cache.
steps: Use 'Forgot Password' on the login screen to reset your credentials.
Clear your browser cache and cookies, then try again.
Ensure you are using the correct email address associated with your NexaFi account.
If your account is locked after multiple failed attempts, wait 30 minutes before retrying.
Contact support if you suspect unauthorized access.
playbook.follow_up_question: Are you seeing a specific error message when you try to log in?

playbook.summary: Pending orders may be waiting for market open, sufficient funds, or manual review.
steps: Check that your order is not a limit order waiting for a specific price.
Confirm you have sufficient buying power in your account.
Market orders placed outside trading hours (9:30am–4pm ET) execute at next market open.
Review your order status under Portfolio > Orders.
If an order has been pending for more than one trading day, contact support.
playbook.follow_up_question: What type of order did you place (market or limit) and when did you place it?
